mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive mean return when both series rise concurrently, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation above 300 on the 1982-1984 index triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are non-negative in over 60 percent of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 300, or the signal reverses direction during disinflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The correlation between INDPRO growth and 20-day forward returns drops below 0.05 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment drop exceeding 100,000 persons signals severe labor market contraction and reduced household income, forcing equity markets lower and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 100000 in a month
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of months following a payroll decline greater than 100,000.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based economic expansion, reducing recession risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns when both GDPC1 and RSAFS grow, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low unemployment indicates capacity utilization is approaching maximums, prompting forward-looking equity investors to bid up prices and generate positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns turn negative when INDPRO rises alongside UNRATE below 4.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power in tight labor regimes.
